author: mark
text:
Audit: bl-05e5 conformance fixtures are faithful to SPEC scenarios

Re-read every test bl-05e5 introduced against the SPEC scenario it claims to cover, asking the bl-fc1d question: does the fixture set up state a real user would arrive in, or state the impl happens to handle?

tests/conformance_moved_clone.rs (3 tests): already remediated by bl-f8f3. seed_moved_clone() now leaves the breadcrumb at the OLD clone path post-rename — the real-world signal — instead of pre-rewriting it to match the buggy impl's expectation. All three subtests use this fixed fixture. Faithful.

tests/conformance_prime_migrate.rs (5 bl-05e5 tests): legacy_clone() runs bl init, which still writes the legacy layout pre-Phase-1B — a real legacy clone, not a pre-rigged one. prime warning/migrate/doctor tests assert against the actual emission paths (Store::discover → emit_legacy_warning → warning_line, migrate::run gated on is_legacy). The 'already xdg = noop' fixture reaches XDG via the real migrate verb rather than by hand-seeding XDG state; its 'HEAD unchanged' assertion is narrow but covers the migrate verb's only main-branch mutation. Faithful.

tests/doctor.rs (modifications): bl-05e5 retitled several existing tests to expect the new 'legacy layout in use' line that always fires on a fresh bl init. Each retitle preserves the original invariant (no orphans, no stale claims, etc) and adds the new finding. Test-expectation adjustments tracking real impl behavior — not the audited class.

src/doctor_moved_tests.rs, src/repair_rebind_tests.rs (unit tests bl-05e5 shipped): these were the unit-level coauthored cases bl-f8f3 fixed alongside the impl. Both now seed phantom recorded paths via a seed_breadcrumb() helper that models gone-after-mv. Faithful.

src/clone_breadcrumb_tests.rs, src/legacy_layout_tests.rs (bl-05e5 unit tests): small API roundtrip/edge-case tests. Not the audited class.

Conclusion: no follow-up bug balls. The class-of-bug risk was confined to the one case bl-f8f3 already remediated.
